summary: Small single-story rectangular building with raised stepped brick gable roof, light-colored corner pillars and band, sandstone lower walls with a continuous row of glass windows, and a surrounding low white border placed on flattened dirt. Roof has stone slab accents at the short ends and a small central chimney-like stack.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box core with stepped gable roof

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: corner pillars / top band (appears smooth white in screenshots, possibly quartz)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof edge accents / small slabs on gable steps

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: uncertain - not observed, included as low confidence candidate

elements: continuous horizontal window band on long sides (3-4 panes per side)
prominent stepped brick gable roof with multiple terraces
small central chimney/stack on roof ridge
corner vertical white blocks forming frame
surrounding white low border/walkway around building on dirt pad
rebuild_hints: Footprint: rectangular core approximately 7x11 blocks (estimate based on top views) with the long sides having a 3-4 block wide window band.
Walls: lower ~2 blocks of sandstone, then a 1-block-high white band (quartz or smooth stone) forming a continuous top course and corner pillars rising to roofline.
Windows: place contiguous glass panes in a horizontal row centered on the wall, separated by minimal vertical supports (corner pillars occupy ends).
Roof: build stepped brick layers ascending to a central ridge; each step inset one block from the previous; add stone slabs at the short-end steps and a small 1x1 chimney stack at center.
Perimeter: add a single-block-high white border around the building footprint, set on the flattened dirt area; building sits recessed slightly within a larger dirt trench.
uncertainties: Exact block IDs for the white smooth material: appears like quartz or smooth stone; labeled as stonebrick in schema but confidence is moderate.
Precise footprint dimensions: estimated from top-down images; count uncertain by +/-1-2 blocks in each direction.
Interior layout not visible; floor height and interior partitions are unknown.
Lower wall material appears as sandstone texture but could be another yellowish stone variant; confidence is high but not certain.
Whether the white border around the building is one-block thick all around or includes slabs is partially ambiguous from angles.
